rationale: sys.argv[1] is attacker-controlled when invoked from a setuid wrapper or untrusted caller. Flows directly into f-string then subprocess.call(shell=True), no validator. Standard CodeQL py/command-line-injection misses this because its source model is RemoteFlowSource only; RAPTOR's LocalFlowSource pack widens the source model to include commandargs and catches it. Real CWE-78.
